prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model. Historical daily logarithmic returns for DIS over the past year were analyzed to calculate the daily drift (average return) and daily volatility (standard deviation of returns). These parameters were then used to project the stock price for 20 days into the future, assuming the price follows a log-normal distribution. The point estimate represents the median of this projected distribution, while the confidence intervals are derived from the corresponding quantiles, reflecting the expected range of price movements based on historical volatility.
